name: Iterators.Operators

documentation.description:
Operators are chainable functions (monads) that take an iterator and
return another iterator. The ones in this package transform the type
they take into the one they generate. Thus, using any of these operators
produces a read-only sequence, since the root container cursor cannot be
preserved.

tagged_types.name: Chain
tagged_types.code: type Chain is new Into.Iterator with private;

tagged_types.name: Operator
tagged_types.code: type Operator is abstract new Into.Iterator with private;

documentation.description: Operators that transform from one type into another.

subprograms.name: As_Iterator
subprograms.code: function As_Iterator (This : in out Chain) return Into.Iterator_Reference

subprograms.name: Concatenate
subprograms.code:
function Concatenate (L : From.Iterator'Class;
                      R : Operator'Class) return Into.Iterator'Class

documentation.description:
Function to build a chain of iterator & operators.
Intended to be renamed as "&" in Linkers packages.

subprograms.name: Continue
subprograms.code:
procedure Continue (This : in out Chain;
                    Last :        Operator'Class)

documentation.description:
This presumes that This already contains a chain, and Last is going
to become the bottom of the chain, stored in this operator.

subprograms.name: First
subprograms.code: function First (This : in out Chain) return From.Iterator_Reference

subprograms.name: Flat_Map
Flat_Map
For_Each
Last
subprograms.code: function Last (This : in out Chain) return Into.Iterator_Reference

subprograms.name: Map
Map
Next
subprograms.code: function Next (This : in out Chain) return Into.Cursor'Class

subprograms.name: Resume
subprograms.code:
procedure Resume (This : in out Chain;
                  Prev :        From.Iterator'Class)

subprograms.name: Scan
Set_Upstream
subprograms.code:
procedure Set_Upstream (This     : in out Operator;
                        Upstream : From.Iterator'Class)

documentation.description:
Replaces upstream going up the chain to the root upstream that has no
parent yet still is an Operator'Class

subprograms.name: Start
subprograms.code:
procedure Start (This  : in out Chain;
                 First :        From.Iterator'Class)

documentation.description:
Begin a sequence with First at the root. If the sequence was already
started it is reset.

subprograms.name: Upstream
subprograms.code:
function Upstream (This : in out Operator'Class)
                   return From.Iterator_Reference

formals.name: From
formals.code: with package From is new Root (<>);

formals.name: Into
formals.code: with package Into is new Root (<>);
